audit_items.proof_trace: The only pointer access in rpc_client.cc is through `_impl`, a `shared_ptr<RpcClientImpl>` initialized in the constructor initializer list via `new RpcClientImpl(options)`. In C++, `new` either returns a valid non-null pointer or throws `bad_alloc`; it cannot return null. `_impl` is never reset or reassigned anywhere in the file, so every subsequent dereference (`GetOptions`, `ResetOptions`, `ConnectionCount`, `Stop`) operates on a guaranteed non-null smart pointer.
